title: B.Sc. (Hons. with Research) Forensic Science
content: *4-year Undergraduate Degree (Honours with Research) S. No. | Broad Category of Course | Minimum Credit Requirement for a4-Year UG Programme | 1 | Major (Core) | 80 | 2 | Minor Stream | 32 | 3 | Multidisciplinary | 09 | 4 | Ability Enhancement Courses (AEC) | 08 | 5 | Skill Enhancement Courses (SEC) | 09 | 6 | Value Added Courses common for all UG | 06-08 | 7 | Summer Internship | 02-04 | 8 | Research Project/ Dissertation | 12 | 9 | Total | 160 | 4-year UG Degree with Honours (Source: - UGC Curriculum & Credit Framework for Undergraduate Programmes) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. 01 Multidisciplinary Forensic Foundation: Provide rigorous training in core forensic disciplines (biology, chemistry, physics) with emphasis on scientific methods, crime scene investigation techniques, and evidentiary analysis 02 Advanced Research Competency: Develop strong research skills through hands-on projects, dissertation work, and training in modern forensic technologies and analytical techniques 03 Specialized Technical Expertise: Equip students with specialized laboratory skills including DNA analysis, toxicology, fingerprint technology, and digital forensics applications 04 Legal and Ethical Framework: Instill understanding of criminal justice systems, courtroom procedures, and ethical responsibilities in forensic practice and evidence handling 05 Professional Readiness: Prepare graduates for forensic careers or advanced studies through industry collaborations, internships, and development of critical thinking and problem-solving abilities 01 Apply knowledge of basic sciences to the discipline and to provide the solution in the area of forensic sciences 02 Employ critical thinking and the scientific knowledge to design, carry out, record and analyze the results of complex forensic problems. 03 Create an awareness for intricate forensic issues and propagate knowledge for public health and safety, along with the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. 04 Demonstrate the knowledge of forensic science for sustainable development of man, society and environment and provide assistance to the Criminal Justice System and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the society 05 Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the forensic lab professionals or Work proficiently as a member of crime investigating teams 06 Proficiently communicate with the forensic community and being able to understand and write effective reports, documentation and make effective presentations. 07 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the forensic skills and apply these to one’s own work, or as a member in a team, to manage projects. 08 Develop the ability to critically evaluate theories, methods, principles, and applications of pure and applied science in multidisciplinary domain. 01 02 03 01 Exhibit technical skills required for examination of questioned documents and toxicological evidences. 02 Acquire industrial exposure and scientific knowledge through industry internship and research-based learning in State and Central forensic labs. XII with Science with minimum 50% marks Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks.
